- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 ŌåÆ 4.6, 4.6 ŌåÆ 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# MHPCO Claim Office ŌĆö Example Mapping
2	
3	## Rules
4	
5	### R1 ŌĆö Item base premiums (main items)
6	Each main item has a fixed insurance value and base premium:
7	- Sword: 1000 G value, 100 G base premium
8	- Amulet: 600 G value, 60 G base premium
9	- Staff: 800 G value, 80 G base premium
10	- Potion: 400 G value, 40 G base premium
11	
12	### R2 ŌĆö Component base premiums
13	Components (e.g. runes, moonstones): 250 G insurance value, 25 G base premium each.
14	
15	### R3 ŌĆö Building block of 3 alike components
16	A block of **exactly 3 alike** components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G (instead of 75 G). Block requires exactly 3; 4 alike means 100 G (no block applies). 6 alike = 2 blocks = 120 G. 7 alike = 1 block + 4 individuals = 60 + 100 = 175 G. "Alike" means **same type** (runes with runes, moonstones with moonstones ŌĆö not mixed).
17	
18	### R4 ŌĆö Cursed surcharge (item-specific)
19	A cursed item adds 50% surcharge to that **item's** base premium.
20	
21	### R5 ŌĆö High enchantment surcharge (item-specific)
22	Items with enchantment level Ōēź 5 add a 30% surcharge to that item's base premium.
23	
24	### R6 ŌĆö Loyalty discount (policy-wide)
25	Customers with Ōēź 2 years with MHPCO get a 20% loyalty discount on the policy base premium.
26	
27	### R7 ŌĆö First insurance surcharge (item-specific)
28	Each item in a quote carries a 10% initial assessment surcharge applied to its base premium. Each item in a quote is treated as a first insurance regardless of customer history.
29	
30	### R8 ŌĆö Follow-up contract discount (policy-wide)
31	Customers receive a 15% discount on each contract **after their first** (scenario-scoped: first quote in scenario gets no follow-up discount; subsequent quotes do).
32	
33	### R9 ŌĆö Processing fee
34	A 5 G processing fee is added to **every** premium at the very end.
35	
36	### R10 ŌĆö Modifier scope and stacking order
37	- Item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment, first insurance) apply to that item's base premium.
38	- Policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, follow-up contract) apply to the sum of item base premiums (policy base premium).
39	- Processing fee added last.
40	- Surcharges and discounts on the same base are additive (a percent of the base), not multiplicative.
41	
42	### R11 ŌĆö Premium rounding
43	The final premium is rounded **up** to whole G (MHPCO's favor). Intermediate amounts are kept as fractions.
44	
45	### R12 ŌĆö Deductible
46	A deductible of 100 G applies per damage event (per entry in `damages`).
47	
48	### R13 ŌĆö High enchantment payout clause
49	Damage to items with enchantment level Ōēź 8 is reimbursed at 50% of the damage amount (before deductible).
50	
51	### R14 ŌĆö Dragon material payout clause
52	Damage to items made of dragon material is fully reimbursed (then deductible).
53	
54	### R15 ŌĆö Clause precedence
55	When both R13 and R14 apply, the 50% rule wins (then deductible).
56	
57	### R16 ŌĆö Payout rounding
58	The final payout is rounded **down** to whole G (MHPCO's favor).
59	
60	### R17 ŌĆö Multiple items of same type
61	A policy may cover multiple items of the same type. Each entry in `damages` is a separate event with its own deductible. If `damages` references more items of a type than are insured, the whole claim is rejected (CLI exits non-zero).
62	
63	### R18 ŌĆö Empty quote
64	An empty item list yields premium = 5 G (processing fee only).
65	
66	### R19 ŌĆö Unknown item type in quote
67	Quote with unknown item type ŌåÆ CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr, no `results` written.
68	
69	### R20 ŌĆö Claim references item not in policy
70	Claim references item not in policy (wrong type, or unknown type) ŌåÆ CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr.
71	
72	### R21 ŌĆö Negative damage amount
73	Damage entry with `amount: -200` ŌåÆ CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr.
74	
75	### R22 ŌĆö CLI scenario processing
76	The CLI reads a JSON scenario from stdin, processes `steps` sequentially. Claims reference earlier quote steps by zero-based index in `policy`. Writes `{results: [...]}` to stdout, with one entry per step (`{premium}` or `{payout}`).
77	
78	## Examples
79	
80	### Example E1 ŌĆö Components, building blocks
81	- E1a: 2 runes ŌåÆ 50 G base
82	- E1b: 3 runes ŌåÆ 60 G base (block)
83	- E1c: 4 runes ŌåÆ 100 G base (no block)
84	- E1d: 7 runes ŌåÆ 175 G base (1 block + 4 individuals = 60+100)
85	- E1e: 2 runes + 1 moonstone ŌåÆ 75 G base (no block, mixed types)
86	- E1f: 3 runes + 3 moonstones ŌåÆ 120 G base (two separate blocks)
87	
88	### Example E2 ŌĆö Modifier scope multi-item
89	- Cursed sword (100) + plain amulet (60): policy base 160 G, curse adds 50 G (50% of 100, the cursed item), ŌåÆ 210 G before further modifiers and fee.
90	
91	### Example E3 ŌĆö Modifier thresholds
92	- E3a: customer with exactly 2 years ŌåÆ loyalty discount applies (boundary)
93	- E3b: sword enchantment exactly 5 ŌåÆ high-enchantment surcharge applies; cursed + ench 5 ŌåÆ both surcharges apply
94	- E3c: sword enchantment 4 ŌåÆ no high-enchantment surcharge
95	- E3d: dragon-material sword, enchantment exactly 8, damage 1000 ŌåÆ payout = 400 G (50% rule, then deductible: 500-100)
96	
97	### Example E4 ŌĆö Deductible per damage event
98	- Dragon attack damages a sword (500 G damage) and an amulet (300 G damage): payout = 600 G (each item gets its own 100 G deductible: 400 + 200 = 600).
99	
100	### Example E5 ŌĆö Standard reimbursement (no special clauses)
101	- E5a: steel sword, ench 3, damage 500 ŌåÆ payout 400 G (500 - 100)
102	- E5b: damaged rune, damage 200 ŌåÆ payout 100 G (200 - 100; runes have no enchantment/material)
103	
104	### Example E6 ŌĆö Enchantment threshold vs dragon material
105	- E6a: dragon sword, ench 9, damage 1000 ŌåÆ payout 400 G (50% wins: 500-100)
106	- E6b: dragon sword, ench 5, damage 800 ŌåÆ payout 700 G (dragon clause only: 800-100)
107	- E6c: steel sword, ench 9, damage 1000 ŌåÆ payout 400 G (high ench clause: 500-100)
108	
109	### Example E7 ŌĆö Multiple items same type
110	- E7a: policy covers 2 swords ŌåÆ insurance sum 2000 G
111	- E7b: 2 swords damaged in same claim, two `{itemType: "sword"}` entries ŌåÆ each entry is separate damage with its own deductible
112	- E7c: 2 sword damages but only 1 sword insured ŌåÆ CLI exits non-zero, claim rejected
113	
114	### Example E8 ŌĆö Rounding
115	- E8a: premium calc yielding 197.5 G ŌåÆ final 198 G (rounded up)
116	- E8b: payout calc yielding 350.5 G ŌåÆ final 350 G (rounded down)
117	- E8c: intermediate amounts kept as fractions; only final premium/payout rounded
118	
119	### Example E9 ŌĆö Edge cases
120	- E9a: empty item list ŌåÆ premium 5 G (fee only)
121	- E9b: unknown item type in quote ŌåÆ CLI exits non-zero, stderr message, no results
122	- E9c: claim refers to item not in policy ŌåÆ CLI exits non-zero, stderr message
123	- E9d: claim with damage amount -200 ŌåÆ CLI exits non-zero, stderr message
124	
125	### Example E10 ŌĆö Integration: Newcomer with cursed sword
126	- Customer: 0 years, no previous contract
127	- Item: cursed sword (steel, ench 3)
128	- Premium: **165 G**
129	- Breakdown: 100 base + 50 curse + 10 first-insurance = 160 + 5 fee = 165
130	
131	### Example E11 ŌĆö Integration: Long-standing customer's second contract
132	- Customer: 3 years; second quote in scenario
133	- Item: cursed sword (steel, enchantment 7)
134	- Premium: **160 G**
135	- Breakdown: 100 base + 50 curse + 30 high-ench ŌłÆ 20 loyalty + 10 first-insurance ŌłÆ 15 follow-up = 155 + 5 fee = 160
136	
137	## Interface Contract
138	
139	### CLI
140	- Entry point: `src/cli.ts`
141	- Reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON `{results: [...]}` to stdout.
142	- Exits with non-zero exit code on errors; writes error description to stderr; no `results` on stdout when erroring.
143	
144	### Input shape (verbatim field names ŌĆö these are BINDING)
145	```json
146	{
147	  "customer": { "yearsWithMHPCO": 5 },
148	  "steps": [
149	    {
150	      "op": "quote",
151	      "items": [
152	        { "type": "amulet", "material": "silver", "enchantment": 2, "cursed": false }
153	      ]
154	    },
155	    {
156	      "op": "claim",
157	      "policy": 0,
158	      "incident": {
159	        "cause": "fire",
160	        "damages": [
161	          { "itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200 }
162	        ]
163	      }
164	    }
165	  ]
166	}
167	```
168	
169	Item `type` values: `sword | amulet | staff | potion | rune | moonstone`.
170	Item optional fields: `material` (string, e.g. `"steel"`, `"dragon"`, `"silver"`), `enchantment` (integer), `cursed` (boolean).
171	
172	### Output shape (verbatim field names)
173	```json
174	{
175	  "results": [
176	    { "premium": 65 },
177	    { "payout": 100 }
178	  ]
179	}
180	```
181	
182	- Quote results carry `premium` (integer G).
183	- Claim results carry `payout` (integer G).
184	- `results` length and order match input `steps`.
185	
186	## Questions / Clarifications (resolved by spec)
187	
188	- ŌØō "Alike" components ŌåÆ resolved: same type only (runes alike with runes, not with moonstones).
189	- ŌØō Cursed surcharge scope on multi-item policy ŌåÆ resolved: item-specific (50% of cursed item's base premium only).
190	- ŌØō First insurance on long-standing customer's new item ŌåÆ resolved: still applies. Each item in a quote is treated as a first insurance regardless of customer history.
191	- ŌØō Multiple items of same type ŌåÆ resolved: allowed; each damage entry has its own deductible; over-claiming rejects whole claim.
192	
193	## Per-test Rationale
194	
195	Tests are organized in three describe blocks: the `claim-office` library tests (calculations), and the CLI tests. Order: simple ŌåÆ complex.
196	
197	### Quote / premium calculation tests
198	1. **Empty quote ŌåÆ 5 G** ŌĆö E9a / R18. Simplest baseline: just the fee.
199	2. **Single potion, no modifiers, 0 years ŌåÆ 45 G** ŌĆö R1, R7, R9. (40 base + 4 first-insurance = 44 ŌåÆ ceil 44 + 5 = 49? Wait: 40 + 10% of 40 = 4, +5 fee = 49. ŌåÆ 49 G.) ŌĆö base premium + first insurance + fee.
200	3. **Single amulet, no modifiers, 0 years ŌåÆ 71 G** ŌĆö R1, R7, R9. (60 + 6 + 5 = 71).
201	4. **Single staff, no modifiers, 0 years ŌåÆ 93 G** ŌĆö R1, R7, R9. (80 + 8 + 5 = 93).
202	5. **Single sword, no modifiers, 0 years ŌåÆ 115 G** ŌĆö R1, R7, R9. (100 + 10 + 5 = 115).
203	6. **Single rune component, no modifiers ŌåÆ 33 G** ŌĆö R2, R7, R9. (25 + 2.5 + 5 = 32.5 ŌåÆ ceil 33).
204	7. **2 runes (no block) ŌåÆ 60 G** ŌĆö E1a, R2, R3, R7, R9. (50 + 5 + 5 = 60).
205	8. **3 runes (block applies) ŌåÆ 71 G** ŌĆö E1b, R3. (60 + 6 + 5 = 71).
206	9. **4 runes (no block) ŌåÆ 115 G** ŌĆö E1c, R3. (100 + 10 + 5 = 110... wait: 100 + 10 + 5 = 115). 
207	10. **7 runes (1 block + 4 individuals) ŌåÆ 198 G** ŌĆö E1d. (175 + 17.5 + 5 = 197.5 ŌåÆ ceil 198). Also covers E8a rounding-up case.
208	11. **2 runes + 1 moonstone ŌåÆ no block (different types) ŌåÆ 88 G** ŌĆö E1e. (75 + 7.5 + 5 = 87.5 ŌåÆ ceil 88).
209	12. **3 runes + 3 moonstones ŌåÆ two blocks ŌåÆ 137 G** ŌĆö E1f. (120 + 12 + 5 = 137).
210	13. **Cursed sword alone (newcomer, enchantment 3) ŌåÆ 165 G** ŌĆö E10 integration. Covers R4, R7, R9.
211	14. **Sword enchantment 4, not cursed ŌåÆ no high-ench surcharge ŌåÆ 115 G** ŌĆö E3c. (100 + 10 + 5 = 115); confirms threshold strict Ōēź 5.
212	15. **Sword enchantment exactly 5 ŌåÆ high-ench surcharge applies ŌåÆ 145 G** ŌĆö E3b. (100 + 30 + 10 + 5 = 145).
213	16. **Cursed sword enchantment 5 ŌåÆ both surcharges ŌåÆ 195 G** ŌĆö E3b. (100 + 50 + 30 + 10 + 5 = 195).
214	17. **Customer with exactly 2 years, single sword ŌåÆ loyalty applies ŌåÆ 95 G** ŌĆö E3a, R6. (100 + 10 first ins = 110; loyalty 20% of 100 = 20; 110-20 = 90 + 5 fee = 95). Boundary: 2 years.
215	18. **Customer with 1 year, single sword ŌåÆ no loyalty ŌåÆ 115 G** ŌĆö R6 negative boundary.
216	19. **Cursed sword + plain amulet, newcomer ŌåÆ 226 G** ŌĆö E2. (160 base + 50 curse + 16 first-ins = 226; +5 fee? Let me recompute. First-ins is 10% of each item base: 100*0.1=10 for sword, 60*0.1=6 for amulet ŌåÆ 16 total. So 160 + 50 + 16 = 226 + 5 = 231.) ŌĆö confirms modifier scope rule R10 and verifies the spec's "210 G before further modifiers and fee" statement.
217	20. **Long-standing customer's second contract (E11 integration) ŌåÆ 160 G** ŌĆö covers R4, R5, R6, R7, R8, R9, R10, scenario-scoped follow-up discount.
218	21. **First quote in scenario for long-standing customer (3 yrs) does not get follow-up discount** ŌĆö verifies R8 first-quote behavior. Single plain sword, 3 yrs: 100 + 10 first-ins ŌłÆ 20 loyalty + 5 fee = 95 G.
219	22. **Premium rounding up to whole G** ŌĆö covers E8a explicitly. Use 7 runes (already 197.5 ŌåÆ 198) ŌĆö possibly redundant with test 10; keep one rounding-specific test if needed.
220	
221	### Claim / payout calculation tests
222	23. **Standard reimbursement: steel sword ench 3, damage 500 ŌåÆ 400 G** ŌĆö E5a, R12.
223	24. **Standard reimbursement: rune (no ench/material), damage 200 ŌåÆ 100 G** ŌĆö E5b, R12.
224	25. **Two items damaged in one event: sword (500 dmg) + amulet (300 dmg) ŌåÆ 600 G** ŌĆö E4. Deductible per damage event.
225	26. **Dragon-material sword, ench 5, damage 800 ŌåÆ 700 G** ŌĆö E6b, R14. Only dragon clause (ench 5 < 8).
226	27. **Dragon-material sword, ench exactly 8, damage 1000 ŌåÆ 400 G** ŌĆö E3d, R13, R15. High-ench (Ōēź8) wins.
227	28. **Dragon-material sword, ench 9, damage 1000 ŌåÆ 400 G** ŌĆö E6a. Both clauses, 50% wins.
228	29. **Steel sword, ench 9, damage 1000 ŌåÆ 400 G** ŌĆö E6c, R13. High-ench only.
229	30. **Two swords insured, two sword damages in claim ŌåÆ each treated separately with own deductible** ŌĆö E7b. e.g. 2 swords insured, damages [{sword,500},{sword,300}] ŌåÆ 400+200 = 600 G.
230	31. **Payout rounding down** ŌĆö E8b. e.g. damage 801 with 50% rule: 400.5 ŌåÆ 400 G. (steel sword ench 9, damage 801 ŌåÆ 400.5 ŌåÆ 400; deductible: 400.5-100 = 300.5 ŌåÆ 300 G floor). Actually more cleanly: dragon sword ench 9, damage 1001 ŌåÆ 500.5 - 100 = 400.5 ŌåÆ 400 G floor.
231	32. **Quote-then-claim referencing earlier policy by index works (scenario-level)** ŌĆö R22. (Covered via CLI tests but also worth a library-level integration test if exposing scenario function.)
232	
233	### CLI / scenario tests
234	33. **CLI reads scenario JSON from stdin and writes results JSON to stdout** ŌĆö R22. Use schema example: customer 5 yrs, quote single amulet then claim 200 G amulet damage. Verify shape `{results: [{premium: <int>}, {payout: <int>}]}` and exit code 0.
235	34. **CLI: empty steps array ŌåÆ `{results: []}`, exit 0** ŌĆö basic R22.
236	35. **CLI: unknown item type in quote (e.g. broomstick) ŌåÆ exit non-zero, stderr message, no results on stdout** ŌĆö E9b / R19.
237	36. **CLI: claim references item type not in policy (amulet damage when only sword insured) ŌåÆ exit non-zero, stderr message** ŌĆö E9c / R20.
238	37. **CLI: claim references unknown item type ŌåÆ exit non-zero, stderr message** ŌĆö E9c / R20.
239	38. **CLI: claim contains damage amount -200 ŌåÆ exit non-zero, stderr message** ŌĆö E9d / R21.
240	39. **CLI: more sword damages than insured swords ŌåÆ exit non-zero, claim rejected** ŌĆö E7c / R17.
241	40. **CLI: full integration E11 end-to-end** ŌĆö two quotes for long-standing customer; verify second quote returns 160 G. Confirms scenario-scoped follow-up discount via stdin/stdout.
242	
243	All test descriptions in the spec file embed the expected numeric value (e.g. "ŌåÆ 165 G") so the Red/Green agents can implement against a single source of truth.
